# The commands below were executed at the start of all sessions to gather context about the environment.

% pwd
/Users/haripritamreddyalapati/github.com/AppointyTech/agent-chat

% ls
daemon-cli
machine-daemon
next-app
README.md
relay

% git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
main

% git status --porcelain
M next-app/components/remote-shell.tsx

2f18788 Refactor dashboard shell UI and add agent config scaffolding
61b176d Add project README
f39e03b Improve relay connection handling
4440d96 Fix daemon download redirects
88bd1ac Fix daemon npm package metadata

% rg --version
ripgrep 15.1.0

% gh --version
gh 2.83.2

% which wget

ffmpeg 8.0.1

Python 3.14.2

/bin/sh: jupyter: command not found


% ls /.dockerenv
ls: /.dockerenv: No such file or directory





IMPORTANT:
- Double check the tools installed in the environment before using them.
- Never call a file editing tool for the same file in parallel.
- Always prefer the Grep, Glob and LS tools over shell commands like find, grep, or ls for codebase exploration.
- Always prefer using the absolute paths when using tools, to avoid any ambiguity.

</system-reminder>

<system-reminder>IMPORTANT: TodoWrite was not called yet. You must call it for any non-trivial task requested by the user. It would benefit overall performance. BYOK Error: 400 {"error":{"message":"level \"minimal\" not supported, valid levels: low, medium, high","type":"invalid_request_error"}}

Troubleshooting tips for custom models:
1. Verify the "provider" type in your BYOK config matches your endpoint (use "openai" for GPT-4o/GPT-5+/o-series/Codex, "anthropic" for Claude, "generic-chat-completion-api" for other OpenAI-compatible APIs)
2. Ensure your baseUrl is reachable and the API key is valid
3. Check that the model name matches what your endpoint expects
